topic: Unit 2: Listening, Reading and Use of Spanish
subject: spanish

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to eat' in Spanish (near future)?
items.why: Students forget the 'a'. The structure is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is essential and links the conjugated ir to the action. Voy comer is ungrammatical.

items.q: How do you say 'I travel by train' in Spanish?
items.why: Students use por or con. Transport always uses EN: en coche, en avión, en autobús, en tren, en barco. Exception: a pie (on foot), a caballo (on horseback). Critical holiday/travel vocab.

items.q: Why does 'inglés' have a written accent?
items.correct: It stresses the final syllable while ending in -s, which is unusual.
items.misconception: All Spanish words have accent marks.
items.why: Students treat accents as random. They follow STRESS RULES: words stressed normally don't need accents; the accent shows when stress falls outside the default position (or distinguishes homophones).

items.q: How many diputados sit in the Congreso?
items.why: Correct answer: 350. “230” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which is the future of 'votar' for 'él'?
items.why: It's votará. “votó” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was the current Spanish Constitution adopted?
items.why: The real figure is 1978. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
